judge_thought:
Explanation: The question asks who was the youngest living person among the presidents listed in the table. To determine this, we need to look at the "age at start date" column, which indicates how old each president was when they became the oldest living president. 

Upon examining the table:
Theodore Roosevelt was 49 years and 241 days old when he became the oldest living president. This is the youngest age among all the presidents listed in the table. 

Other notable ages include:
George Washington: 57 years and 67 days
John Adams: 64 years and 45 days
James Madison: 75 years and 110 days
Herbert Hoover: 58 years and 148 days
Ronald Reagan: 69 years and 349 days
George H. W. Bush: 82 years and 197 days

Since Theodore Roosevelt had the youngest age at the start date, the Prediction Answer is correct.
